description: The Veterinary Medicines Directorate (VMD) protects animal health, public health and the environment. VMD is an executive agency, sponsored by the Department for Environment, Food & Rural Affairs .

details.body:
The Veterinary Medicines Directorate (VMD) protects animal health, public health and the environment.

VMD is an executive agency, sponsored by the Department for Environment, Food & Rural Affairs.

ordered_featured_documents.summary: New drive could help reduce environmental impact
ordered_featured_documents.title: Call for evidence launches on pet flea and tick treatments in UK waterways

ordered_featured_documents.summary: Routine update to adverse event data for L2 and L4 dog vaccines, and reminder for vets to tailor vaccination programmes for individual animals.
ordered_featured_documents.title: VMD reaffirms safety of Leptospira vaccines in dogs

ordered_featured_documents.summary: First Inspections and Enforcement report, covering October and November 2025, providing animal owners, vet practices and other animal health businesses messages on how to remain compliant with the law.
ordered_featured_documents.title: VMD publishes first Inspections and Enforcement report as part of new series

ordered_featured_documents.summary: VMD in-depth assessment confirms continued positive benefit-risk balance for Librela.
ordered_featured_documents.title: Regulator completes in-depth assessment of osteoarthritis pain relief injections for dogs

ordered_featured_documents.summary: Businesses advertising non-medicinal veterinary products to benefit from updated guidance on marketing non-medicinal veterinary products legally.
ordered_featured_documents.title: Regulator publishes updated guidance to help businesses advertise non-medicinal veterinary products legally

ordered_featured_documents.summary: VMD has published webinars to help veterinary professionals, wholesalers and retailers prepare for changes to the supply of veterinary medicines into Northern Ireland.
ordered_featured_documents.title: Webinars on changes to veterinary medicines supply into Northern Ireland from 1 January 2026

ordered_featured_links.title: Veterinary Medicines Registers: manufacturing and distribution

details.secondary_corporate_information_pages: Read about the types of information we routinely publish in our Publication scheme. Read our policy on Social media use.

details.body:
The Chief Executive Officer, VMD provides support and advice to Defra Ministers, the Chief Operating Officer and the Chief Veterinary Officer where the regulation of veterinary medicines contributes to policy in respect of animal health and welfare. They’re supported by the Director of Authorisations and the Director of Operations.

They ensure:

  • the effective implementation of clear operational standards and measures
  • full cost recovery of VMD operations
  • that targets are met

role_appointments.title: Abigail Seager - Chief Executive Officer, VMD

details.body:
Supports the Chief Executive Officer with day-to-day running of the VMD and:

  • Leads the Authorisations Division in delivery of key objectives against published standards and in line with the VMD mission and vision.
  • Sets the strategic direction for the Authorisations Team.
  • Liaises with other Government Departments, CVOs and Ministers as appropriate.
  • Leads on stakeholder engagement with Industry representative bodies, and other global regulators in relation to the authorisation and supply of veterinary medicinal products.
  • Identifies opportunities for global and domestic collaboration.
  • Ensures appropriate budgetary controls are in place within the Authorisations Division, that services offer value for money, and in her absence, deputises for the CEO as the accounting officer.
  • Fulfils the role of Project Leader/Programme Director/SRO as necessary, for example with implementing the Northern Ireland Protocol, and implementing new domestic legislation.
  • Ensures delivery of the VMD science strategy and being the VMD lead in liaison with the Veterinary Products Committee (independent advisory body to the VMD).
  • Takes full and proactive participation at VMD / Defra operational and strategic management meetings.
  • Ensures that the VMD has robust emergency response systems in place for contributing to wider supply chain considerations for veterinary medicines and also has in place mechanisms for dealing with more routine veterinary medicines supply issues.

role.title: Deputy Chief Executive Officer/Director of Authorisations, VMD

role_appointments.title: Gavin Hall - Deputy Chief Executive Officer/Director of Authorisations, VMD

details.body: The VMD Audit and Risk Assurance Committee (ARAC), is a sub-committee of the VMD Management Board (MB). The ARAC supports the Chief Executive as Accounting Officer and the MB in monitoring the corporate governance and control systems in the organisation. It will advise on internal and external audit matters, risk, control and governance in the organisation. The ARAC’s scope and responsibilities include risk management, internal control, governance, financial reporting and internal and external audit.

The VMD Audit and Risk Assurance Committee (ARAC), is a sub-committee of the VMD Management Board (MB). The ARAC supports the Chief Executive as Accounting Officer and the MB in monitoring the corporate governance and control systems in the organisation. It will advise on internal and external audit matters, risk, control and governance in the organisation. The ARAC’s scope and responsibilities include risk management, internal control, governance, financial reporting and internal and external audit.

role_appointments.title: Mike Venables OBE - Non-Executive Director, VMD

details.body: A member of the VMD Management Board and its sub-committee the VMD Audit and Risk Assurance Committee.

details.body:
The Chief Executive Officer, VMD provides support and advice to Defra Ministers, the Chief Operating Officer and the Chief Veterinary Officer where the regulation of veterinary medicines contributes to policy in respect of animal health and welfare. They’re supported by the Director of Authorisations and the Director of Operations.

They ensure:

  • the effective implementation of clear operational standards and measures
  • full cost recovery of VMD operations
  • that targets are met

The Director of Operations, VMD supports the chief executive officer with the day-to-day running of the VMD. They lead on:

  • servicing
  • new policy and legislation
  • support to ministers through briefing and advice on replies to correspondence and Parliamentary Questions
  • pharmacovigilance for veterinary medicines
  • inspection of manufacturers, wholesale dealers and retailers of veterinary medicines
  • enforcement of legislation
  • surveillance for residues of veterinary medicines and banned substances in livestock and animal products

The Director of Authorisations, VMD supports the chief executive officer with the day-to-day running of the VMD and leads on:

  • assessment of applications, issuing and maintenance of Marketing Authorisation
  • licensing of manufacturers and wholesale dealers of veterinary medicines
  • management of the research and development programme on behalf of the policy customer
  • assessing applications for import and export certificates for veterinary medicines

The Chief Executive Officer, VMD provides support and advice to Defra Ministers, the Chief Operating Officer and the Chief Veterinary Officer where the regulation of veterinary medicines contributes to policy in respect of animal health and welfare. They’re supported by the Director of Authorisations and the Director of Operations.

They ensure:

  • the effective implementation of clear operational standards and measures
  • full cost recovery of VMD operations
  • that targets are met

The Director of Authorisations, VMD supports the chief executive officer with the day-to-day running of the VMD and leads on:

  • assessment of applications, issuing and maintenance of Marketing Authorisation
  • licensing of manufacturers and wholesale dealers of veterinary medicines
  • management of the research and development programme on behalf of the policy customer
  • assessing applications for import and export certificates for veterinary medicines

The VMD Audit and Risk Assurance Committee (ARAC), is a sub-committee of the VMD Management Board (MB). The ARAC supports the Chief Executive as Accounting Officer and the MB in monitoring the corporate governance and control systems in the organisation. It will advise on internal and external audit matters, risk, control and governance in the organisation. The ARAC’s scope and responsibilities include risk management, internal control, governance, financial reporting and internal and external audit.
A member of the VMD Management Board and its sub-committee the VMD Audit and Risk Assurance Committee.

Timothy Chairs the VMD Audit and Risk Assurance Committee, a sub-committee of the VMD Management Board and is a member of the Management Board (MB). The VMD Audit and Risk Assurance Committee (ARAC) supports the Chief Executive as Accounting Officer and the MB in monitoring the corporate governance and control systems in the organisation. It will advise on internal and external audit matters, risk, control and governance in the organisation. The ARAC’s scope and responsibilities include risk management, internal control, governance, financial reporting and internal and external audit.

The VMD Audit and Risk Assurance Committee (ARAC), is a sub-committee of the VMD Management Board (MB). The ARAC supports the Chief Executive as Accounting Officer and the MB in monitoring the corporate governance and control systems in the organisation. It will advise on internal and external audit matters, risk, control and governance in the organisation. The ARAC’s scope and responsibilities include risk management, internal control, governance, financial reporting and internal and external audit.
A member of the VMD Management Board and its sub-committee the VMD Audit and Risk Assurance Committee.

Supports the Chief Executive Officer with day-to-day running of the VMD and:

  • Leads the Authorisations Division in delivery of key objectives against published standards and in line with the VMD mission and vision.
  • Sets the strategic direction for the Authorisations Team.
  • Liaises with other Government Departments, CVOs and Ministers as appropriate.
  • Leads on stakeholder engagement with Industry representative bodies, and other global regulators in relation to the authorisation and supply of veterinary medicinal products.
  • Identifies opportunities for global and domestic collaboration.
  • Ensures appropriate budgetary controls are in place within the Authorisations Division, that services offer value for money, and in her absence, deputises for the CEO as the accounting officer.
  • Fulfils the role of Project Leader/Programme Director/SRO as necessary, for example with implementing the Northern Ireland Protocol, and implementing new domestic legislation.
  • Ensures delivery of the VMD science strategy and being the VMD lead in liaison with the Veterinary Products Committee (independent advisory body to the VMD).
  • Takes full and proactive participation at VMD / Defra operational and strategic management meetings.
  • Ensures that the VMD has robust emergency response systems in place for contributing to wider supply chain considerations for veterinary medicines and also has in place mechanisms for dealing with more routine veterinary medicines supply issues.
